title: cordyline stricta
intro: Cordyline stricta, also known as cabbage tree or ti kōuka, is a type of flowering plant native to New Zealand and Australia, characterized by its distinctive red or purple flowers and edible leaves. The plant has been traditionally used in medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which are attributed to the presence of compounds such as flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Cordyline stricta include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and exhibit antioxidant properties. Cordyline stricta is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, where its leaves and roots are combined with other herbs to create remedies for various health conditions.
